full_response:
Let the Earth (the radio transmitter) be at the origin of an inertial frame S.  
Choose the x–axis along the straight-line trajectory of the spacecraft and the y–axis so that the
closest approach occurs at (x=0 , y=L).  
The ship moves with constant speed v ( β = v/c = ½ ) in the +x direction, so in S

 x(t) = v t , y(t)=L , z(t)=0     (1)

with t=0 at the instant of closest approach.  
The astronauts’ proper time τ is related to the Earth–frame time t by

 dτ = dt / γ ⇒ τ = t/γ  (γ ≡ 1/√(1-β²)=2/√3)    (2)

-------------------------------------------------
1.  Doppler formula in the transmitter’s frame  
For a source at rest and a moving observer the relativistic Doppler-shift is

 f obs = γ f src (1 – β cosθ) ,    (3)

where θ is the angle, measured in the Earth frame, between the observer’s velocity v⃗ and the propagation
direction of the light (the line from the source to the observer).

-------------------------------------------------
2.  The instantaneous angle θ  
The unit vector that points from the Earth to the spacecraft is

 d̂ = (x(t) , L , 0)/r(t) ,  r(t)=√[(v t)² + L²] .    (4)

The velocity of the spacecraft is v⃗ = v êx, hence

 cosθ = (v⃗·d̂)/v = x(t)/r(t) = v t / r(t).    (5)

Substituting x(t)=v t gives

 cosθ(t) = β c t / √(β² c² t² + L²).    (6)

-------------------------------------------------
3.  Express cosθ in terms of the astronauts’ proper time τ  
Using t = γ τ from (2):

 cosθ(τ) = β γ c τ / √(β² γ² c² τ² + L²).    (7)

-------------------------------------------------
4.  Observed frequency as a function of τ  
Insert (7) into the Doppler formula (3):

 f(τ) = γ f₀ [ 1 – β·cosθ(τ) ]                     (8)

   = γ f₀
          ⎡ 1 – β · ( β γ c τ )
                / √( β² γ² c² τ² + L² ) ⎤.   (9)

Equation (9) is the exact frequency that must be dialled, expressed with c restored.

-------------------------------------------------
5.  Putting in the numerical value β = ½ , γ = 2/√3  

β² γ = (½)²·(2/√3) = 1 /(2√3) ,

so

 f(τ)= (2/√3) f₀
        ⎡ 1 – ( c τ /(2√3) )
             / √( c² τ²/3 + L² ) ⎤.   (10)

-------------------------------------------------
6.  A compact, scale–free form  
Define the proper–time scale

 τ_L ≡ L /(β γ c) = √3 L / c,    (11)

and the dimensionless variable u ≡ τ/τ_L.  Then (10) becomes

 f(τ) = γ f₀ [ 1 – β u /√(1+u²) ],   γ = 2/√3 , β = ½.  (12)

-------------------------------------------------
7.  Final result  

For a 700-kHz station the astronauts must tune their receiver to

 f(τ)= γ f₀  1 – β² γ c τ /√(β² γ² c² τ² + L²) 

  = 700 kHz × (2/√3)
           1 – ( c τ /(2√3) ) / √( c² τ²/3 + L² ) ,

where τ is the proper time elapsed on board since the moment of closest approach and L is the closest-approach distance.  

(At τ=0 this gives f=γf₀≈8.08×10² kHz; as τ→−∞ it tends to γf₀(1+β)≈1.21 MHz, and as τ→+∞ to γf₀(1−β)≈0.404 MHz.)

\boxed{\,f(\tau)=\gamma\,f_{0}\!\left[\,1-\dfrac{\beta^{2}\gamma\,c\,\tau}
{\sqrt{\beta^{2}\gamma^{2}c^{2}\tau^{2}+L^{2}}}\right]\!,
\qquad\text{with } \beta=\tfrac12,\;\gamma=\tfrac{2}{\sqrt3},\;f_{0}=700\;\text{kHz}\,}

Confidence: 0.67
